task: Identify the makers of the four antique cast-iron desk seal presses in the user-supplied photograph.

config.note: Python pipeline not executed; .env absent. Grok rate-limited, so Claude served as Grok and as proxy for the other API-gated models, using WebSearch/WebFetch for real grounding.

reconnaissance.claude_r1_summary: Devices are screw-driven cast-iron C-scroll desk embossers, c.1850-1910. ChatGPT candidates suspect — Evens & Watson is Philadelphia stove maker, not seal press. Push primary sources: officemuseum.com, norcalseals.weebly.com, Crider/Grampp/Gonty.

threads.hypothesis: Screw-with-return-spring form is NOT Platt Evens percussion and NOT B.B. Hill lever-cam — ChatGPT category-errored
Lowell & Senter (Portland ME, 1837-1866) is a real candidate for the ornate top-left press
Merriam & Co. Boston (Joseph H. Merriam diesinker) is strongest candidate for ornate gilt examples
ChatGPT's 'Evens & Watson (Philadelphia)' is a hallucination — conflating the stove co. with Cincinnati's Platt Evens Jr.
The four presses share form but not maker — attribution-by-image is impossible without maker's marks

findings: Patent US 47,821 (B.B. Hill, Chicopee MA, May 23 1865) is a LEVER-CAM press with fly-leaf + spindle + cam + spring — NOT the screw form pictured.
Platt Evens Jr. (Cincinnati) patented 1854 percussion press jointly with Charles Francis Hall after Foster/Cranch dispute — all three made percussion, none made screw.
Lowell & Senter exhibited seal presses at 1839 Boston MCMA; primary business was silverware/jewelry/nautical instruments; 1866 fire destroyed Portland store; firm dissolved ~1869.
Merriam & Co. (Boston) = Joseph H. Merriam, die-sinker; known for figural presses (Toad 1863, Eagle 1865, Lion 1869 via Carsley patent D3363). Plain C-scroll bodies also likely sold.
C.H. Morse Stamp Co., Rochester NY, founded 1863, still operating as NY Marking Devices Corp.
Reference bible: 'Fancy, Figural and Unusual Seal Presses: A Collector's Guide' — Crider, Grampp & Gonty, 2nd ed. 240+ body styles. CGG body-style numbers used for ID.
Don Grampp dating method: flat base (pre-1860), single pour hole (1860+), double pour hole (later 19th c.), large indentations (late 19th-20th c.).

top_left.note: Heavy gold-on-black floral japanning matches mid-Victorian Boston/Portland tradition.

top_right.note: Plainer cast, thick base — later Industrial-era form.

top_candidates: polished cast iron of same family
continental European (Jarrett London, or French unmarked)
bottom_right.note: Brass/bronze appearance is the most distinctive clue and the least diagnostic.

corrections_to_chatgpt: Evens & Watson (Philadelphia) is a stove foundry, not a seal press maker. The real firm is Platt Evens Jr. of Cincinnati (percussion, not screw).
Illinois Iron & Bolt Company — no primary-source evidence for seal presses.
Adam Ramage, George E. Clymer — printing/hand-press inventors, not in the seal-press lineage.
B.B. Hill patent is for a lever-cam mechanism, not the screw press pictured.
synthesis.grok_blind_spot: Treating four distinct presses as one identification problem. They share a FORM, not a MAKER. ChatGPT's confident attributions are model hallucinations that must not propagate.
